divergence: the camera was never invented — weddings hire sketch artists, driver's licenses carry portraits, and vacations end at the easel
story_angle: consumer report reviewing the apocalypse's amenities, with star rating
headline: DMV Rolls Out Mandatory Star Ratings for Intergalactic Sketch Services Amid Junk Plague
deck: Consumers now required to rate their charcoal ID sketchers while fighting for their lives—literally.
article:
The Department of Motor Visuals has imposed star ratings on every intergalactic sketch delivery. Citizens dodging incoming circulars must now pause long enough to score their portraitist or face fines.

Vicomte LeFacteur’s empty postbox earned three stars last cycle after repeated delays. Commissioner Eloise Charcoile called the lag unacceptable, noting that late sketches leave residents without usable IDs during active debris events.

Tariffs on imported pads and Canadian artists have raised costs for households already required by local rules to submit fresh plague-circular portraits each week. Many report stockpiling charcoal and bracing for higher fees.

Self-portraits are spreading as a workaround. Residents are advised to keep supplies ready; any sketch that fails to arrive on schedule drags the rating down and risks license revocation.
author: Patricia Doodlebrush, Bureau Chief of Circular Affairs

weather.condition: Junk debris drifting at sketch height; carry spare charcoal

oped.title: Star Ratings Preserve Artistic Dignity Amid the Junk Scourge
oped.author: Baron Von Smudge, Emeritus Professor of Smudged Proportions and Former DMV Sketch Czar
oped.body:
The five-star rule is not oppression; it is basic accountability. While citizens evade flying debris they are still expected to credit the artists who record their features. Complaints about shaky hands or squinting during an assault ignore the fact that low scores punish the sketcher, not the subject.

My own renewal received two stars solely because my homburg threw an awkward shadow; the rater clearly needed instruction in chiaroscuro. Four stars should be the baseline. Five stars belong only to those who finish the portrait while debris passes overhead. Anything lower simply subsidizes mediocrity.

classifieds.text: Intergalactic DMV outpost seeks charcoal sketch artists who can capture likenesses mid-junk-plague assault while clients scribble 5-star ratings; must own riot easel and tolerate zero-tolerance feedback.
Retiring sketch service selling 47 lightly used intergalactic easels, crates of emergency charcoal, and one-star rating shields after DMV mandate forced early closure amid daily plague ambushes.
Lost: my intergalactic driver's license sketch during rating scuffle at Sector 7 DMV; reward offered for its return before junk plague claims the last remaining portrait of my third eye.
Desperate apology to the sketch artist I gave two stars yesterday—your likeness was fine, the junk swarm was not; willing to pose again under safer conditions, no ratings required.

panels.caption: DMV clerk slaps a tablet on the counter as junk plague spores eat the desk: "Rate your intergalactic sketch artist before the next wave hits."
Bureaucrat leans in while the artist frantically sketches the customer's dissolving face: "Lower than four stars and we void the portrait—no exceptions during plague season."
Customer, half-eaten, gives one star as the sketch melts: "At least the plague's consistent."

joke.setup: DMV now requires star ratings for intergalactic sketch artists to fight the junk plague on driver portraits.
joke.punchline: Four stars just means the artist remembered you have two eyes instead of mistaking you for a melting traffic cone.
joke.text: DMV now requires star ratings for intergalactic sketch artists to fight the junk plague on driver portraits. Four stars just means the artist remembered you have two eyes instead of mistaking you for a melting traffic cone.

sponsor_ads.headline: DMV-Approved 5-Star Sketch Passports
sponsor_ads.body: Tired of intergalactic checkpoints rejecting your 1-star junk portrait? Our certified easel team delivers mandatory-rated likenesses that survive any nebula border.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Expires when the artist does. No camera refunds.

sponsor_ads.headline: Beat the Junk Plague: Sketch Rating Insurance
sponsor_ads.body: Protect your driver's license from downgrades under the new star system—our policy replaces low-rated portraits with DMV-vetted alternatives before your next commute.

sponsor_ads.headline: Intergalactic Vacation Sketches, Pre-Rated
sponsor_ads.body: Return from Mars with easel art that already carries 4.9 stars, dodging the plague of unverified junk that clogs family albums.
sponsor_ads.tagline: Vacations end at the easel—ratings start there.

sponsor_ads.headline: Wedding Sketch Artists: Mandatory Star Edition
sponsor_ads.body: Hire plague-resistant illustrators whose work meets DMV intergalactic thresholds, ensuring your marriage certificate doesn't get flagged for low likeness quality.

stories.headline: Charcoal Cartel Raises Prices After DMV Ties Artist Pay to Junk-Plague Survival Scores
stories.deck: Suppliers now bill extra for plague-resistant smocks rated four stars or higher
stories.article:
The American Charcoal Consortium posted a seventeen percent revenue jump after DMV offices began tying artist pay to customer ratings collected during active circular assaults. New contracts require suppliers to furnish smocks tested against low-velocity nebula debris.

Regional branches must sustain three-star averages or forfeit federal sketch-service funds, driving bulk orders that further lifted supplier margins.

real_world_echo: Trump 50% tariff on Canadian imports, mirrored via new duties on imported sketch pads and Canadian portrait artists to 'protect' domestic circular delivery during the plague
